info.title: Whats Good API
info.description:
Manage your event pipeline: add **sources** to scrape, review incoming **events**, approve the ones you want, and read back your published feed.

## Authentication
Send your API key in the **`X-API-Key`** header (or `?api_key=` query param) on every request:

```
curl https://whatsgoodapi.up.railway.app/me -H "X-API-Key: YOUR_KEY"
```

Every request operates on **your account** — you never pass an account id, it's inferred from your key.

## Workflow
1. `GET /me` — confirm your account
2. `POST /sources` — add a source (this starts a scrape)
3. `GET /events?status=new` — review what was found
4. `POST /events/approve` — approve a batch
5. `GET /events?status=approved&upcoming=true` — your live feed

Events move `new → approved | rejected`. Approving an event publishes it to your feed.

post.description: Add a source. Creating it immediately starts a scrape in the background; new events arrive with status `new`.

put.description: Update one of your sources. Send only the fields you want to change.

delete.description: Delete a source. By default its events are kept; pass `clear_events=true` to delete them too.

parameters.name: clear_events

schema.description: Delete associated events instead of nulling source_id
schema.title: Clear Events

parameters.description: Delete associated events instead of nulling source_id

post.description: Run a source's scraper now. Returns a background task you can poll via `GET /tasks/{task_id}`.

get.description: List a source's run history (scrape counts, status, errors).

parameters.name: limit

schema.description: Maximum number of logs to return
schema.title: Limit

parameters.description: Maximum number of logs to return

get.description: Get a single source run log.

post.description: Manually add a single event. It enters with status `new` for review.

get.description: List your events with filtering and pagination. Use `status=approved&upcoming=true` for your live feed.

parameters.name: upcoming

schema.description: Only events starting now or in the future
schema.title: Upcoming

parameters.description: Only events starting now or in the future

post.description: Approve multiple events at once. Returns which ids were approved; unknown ids come back under `not_found`.

get.description: Summary counts of your events (by status, source, category, upcoming, and more).
Get one of your events in full.

put.description: Edit one of your events. `status` must be `new`, `approved`, or `rejected`.

post.description: Mark an event as a duplicate of another of your events.
Approve an event (status → `approved`). Approved events appear under `GET /events?status=approved`.

get.description: List the event categories that appear in your events.
List the available audience types you can assign to events.
Return your account details (city, branding, settings).
Check the status of a background task (e.g. a source run).

BulkApproveRequest.title: BulkApproveRequest
BulkApproveRequest.description: Request to approve many events at once.

BulkApproveResponse.title: BulkApproveResponse
BulkApproveResponse.description: Result of approving multiple events. `approved` lists the ids that were set to approved; `not_found` lists ids that do not exist in your account.

source.title: Source
source.description: Origin tag for the event; defaults to 'manual'.

EventCreate.title: EventCreate
EventCreate.description: Fields for manually adding a single event. Only `title` is required; the new event enters with status `new` for review. Classification (category/audience) and status are set later during review.

EventUpdate.title: EventUpdate
EventUpdate.description: Schema for updating an event (manual corrections).

MarkDuplicateRequest.title: MarkDuplicateRequest
MarkDuplicateRequest.description: Request to mark event as duplicate.

ScraperLogListResponse.title: ScraperLogListResponse
ScraperLogListResponse.description: Response schema for paginated list of scraper logs.

ScraperLogResponse.title: ScraperLogResponse
ScraperLogResponse.description: Response schema for scraper log entries.
